seoTitle: How to Change Font in Microsoft Forms Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to use the inline rich text toolbar in Microsoft Forms to adjust text size, color, and formatting for questions and instructions.
intro: Making a key question stand out or clarifying important instructions in a survey relies on Microsoft Forms' inline text formatting tools rather than a global font swap, since the core interface font stays locked for accessibility. This guide shows how to highlight text inside a question box to trigger the floating formatting toolbar, then adjust size, color, and style. You'll also learn how to apply a broader theme through the Style pane.

steps.heading: Click into a text field
steps.body: Select any question title, description box, or header block where you want to adjust the text.

steps.heading: Highlight your text
steps.body: Use your cursor to select the specific words or phrases you want to restyle.

steps.heading: Use the floating toolbar
steps.body: A rich text formatting toolbar appears above your selection, letting you adjust font size between small, medium, and large presets.

steps.heading: Apply color and formatting
steps.body: Select a high-visibility accent color from the palette, and use inline shortcuts like bold or italics to emphasize key phrases.

steps.heading: Check the overall theme
steps.body: Tap the Style paint roller icon in the top-right toolbar to browse visual theme layouts, then click Preview to verify how everything looks to respondents.

faq.question: Can I change the entire form's font family in Microsoft Forms?
faq.answer: No, Forms locks its core interface typeface to standard system fonts for accessibility and responsiveness, but you can adjust size, color, and style inline.

faq.question: How do I make a specific question stand out?
faq.answer: Highlight the text and use the floating rich text toolbar to bump up the font size or apply a bold, high-contrast color.

faq.question: Does formatting look the same on mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Yes, the rendering engine automatically scales your inline text formatting across both mobile and desktop recipient views.

faq.question: Where do I preview my formatting changes?
faq.answer: Click the Preview button at the top of the canvas to see exactly how your formatted text will appear to respondents.
